name: Fermyon Spin
pricing_notes: Spin framework open source; Fermyon Cloud: Free tier (5 apps, 100K requests/mo); Starter $8/mo

auth.notes: Local spin dev needs no auth; Fermyon Cloud deployment uses spin login (GitHub OAuth)

performance.notes: Coldstart near-zero (<1ms for WASM vs 100ms+ for container runtimes); HTTP triggers measured in microseconds

agent_readiness.notes: WebAssembly serverless framework. Near-zero coldstart. Multi-language support (Rust, JS/TS, Python, Go). Key-value store, SQLite, object storage components built-in. Excellent security isolation via WASM sandbox.

gotchas: WASM sandbox limits available libraries (no arbitrary native dependencies)
Rust gives best performance; Python support more limited
Fermyon Cloud less mature than Cloudflare/Fastly infrastructure
Long-running compute not suited to serverless pattern
best_when: Edge functions requiring near-zero coldstart (IoT, real-time triggers)
Security-sensitive workloads (WASM sandbox isolation stronger than containers)
Polyglot serverless (Rust + JS + Python from same platform)
Agents needing sandboxed tool execution with hardware-level isolation
avoid_when: Need arbitrary native library support (pip install anything)
Teams needing largest edge network (use Cloudflare Workers)
summary: Fermyon Spin is a WebAssembly serverless framework with near-zero coldstart times. Supports Rust, JavaScript, Python, Go compiled to WASM. WASM sandbox provides stronger security isolation than containers. Built-in key-value store, SQLite, and object storage components. Best for edge functions and agent tool sandboxing where security isolation matters.
